text.title: chunking-strategies Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for chunking-strategies as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Metadata loss during chunking: chunker drops source file path or page number.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Citations in the final RAG response will be wrong. Always verify metadata propagation through the full ingestion pipeline before indexing.

failure-mode.description: Overlap causes duplicate retrieval: top-k=20 returns 5 overlapping windows of the same paragraph.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: LLM receives 5x the same content; responses are padded with repetition rather than diverse evidence. Deduplicate by source range before passing to reranker.

failure-mode.description: Semantic chunking embedding model mismatch: using text-embedding-3-small for chunking but text-embedding-3-large for retrieval.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Similarity thresholds calibrated in one vector space are meaningless in another. Use the same model for both ingestion and retrieval.

failure-mode.description: Rebuilding without re-chunking: changing chunk strategy but leaving old chunks in the same index.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Old and new chunks in the same index cause inconsistent retrieval behavior. Always rebuild the full index after changing chunking strategy.
